title: Analytics Parameter
description: Defines a single analytics event parameter with type, validation rules, and metadata.

type.title: Parameter Type
type.description: The data type of the parameter. Supports Dart primitive types and type aliases.

description.title: Description
description.description: Human-readable description of the parameter's purpose.
examples: The unique identifier for the user session
Screen name where the event occurred

identifier.title: Code Identifier
identifier.description: Override the Dart code identifier (method parameter name). Defaults to the YAML key name. Must be valid snake_case when enforce_snake_case_parameters is enabled.

param_name.title: Wire Name
param_name.description: Override the analytics key sent to providers. Defaults to the YAML key name.

dart_type.title: Dart Type Override
dart_type.description: Explicitly set the Dart type (e.g., a custom enum type). The generator will use this type in the method signature and serialize it using `.name`. Cannot be combined with allowed_values.

import.title: Dart Import Path
import.description: Custom import path for the dart_type. Required when dart_type references a type from another package or file.

allowed_values.title: Allowed Values
allowed_values.description: Restricts the parameter to a fixed set of values. The list type must match the parameter type. Cannot be combined with dart_type.

regex.title: Regex Pattern
regex.description: Regex pattern for validation. Used in generated code for runtime validation. Cannot contain triple quotes (''').

min.title: Minimum Value
min.description: Minimum value for numeric parameters (int, double, num).

max.title: Maximum Value
max.description: Maximum value for numeric parameters (int, double, num).

meta.title: Metadata
meta.description: Custom metadata for this parameter (e.g., ownership, Jira tickets, team).

operations.title: Operations
operations.description: List of operations supported by this parameter. Only used for context properties (user properties, etc.).

items.x-note: The parser accepts any string; this enum reflects the documented operations from the specification.

added_in.title: Added In Version
added_in.description: Version when this parameter was added. Used for documentation and tracking.

deprecated_in.title: Deprecated In Version
deprecated_in.description: Version when this parameter was deprecated. Used for documentation and tracking.

additionalProperties.description: Legacy type-as-key syntax: the parameter type can be specified as a YAML key instead of using the 'type' field. E.g., `my_param: { string: null, description: '...' }` is equivalent to `my_param: { type: string, description: '...' }`. Any key not matching a known property name is treated as the type.

x-nullable-suffix.description: Appending '?' to the type or dart_type string marks the parameter as nullable. E.g., 'string?' means a nullable string, 'PaymentMethod?' means a nullable custom type.

x-shorthand.description: A parameter can be defined as a simple string value (just the type) instead of a full object. E.g., `session_id: string` is equivalent to `session_id: { type: string }`. A null value references a shared parameter.

x-legacy-type-key.description: When the 'type' key is not present, the parser searches for any key that is not a known property (description, allowed_values, identifier, param_name, dart_type, meta, regex, min_length, max_length, min, max, operations, added_in, deprecated_in, import) and uses it as the type. If no such key is found, defaults to 'dynamic'.
